{{Quote|At approximately 3900 BBY, Queen Tasia sponsored one of the last of her world's colonization missions. The three ships—''Beneficient Tasia'', ''Constant'', and ''Mother Vima''—departed Grizmallt and vanished from sight.|A Galactic Alliance historical document|<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>}}
'''''Mother Vima''''' was a [[Colony ship/Legends|colony ship]] that, along with ''[[Constant]]'', served as one of two support [[Starship/Legends|starships]] of the ''[[Beneficent Tasia]]''. The three vessels departed the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Grizmallt/Legends|Grizmallt]] on an expedition to the dangerous southern quadrant of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]<ref name="GB" /> in approximately [[3900 BBY/Legends|3900 BBY]].<ref name="NEC" /> The starships were filled with [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Refugee/Legends|refugees]] who hoped to find fortune and glory<ref name="GB" /> while escaping a [[Grizmallti civil war/Legends|civil war]] on Grizmallt.<ref name="Nexus">''[[Nexus of Power]]''</ref> The expedition was led by the [[Scout/Legends|scout]] [[Kwilaan/Legends|Kwilaan]],<ref name="Secrets">''[[Secrets of Naboo]]''</ref> had the sponsorship of Grizmallt's [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Elsinoré den Tasia]], and was blessed by a [[Unidentified famous Jedi Master|famous Jedi Master]].<ref name="GB" />

After Grizmallt lost contact with the ''Beneficent Tasia'', the [[Grizmallti/Legends|Grizmallti]] considered the expedition a failure. In reality, the vessels had become damaged and unable to make contact, and they ultimately landed on the planet [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]].<ref name="GB" /> The refugees,<ref name="Nexus" /> which included a member of [[House Korden]],<ref name="Galaxy of Intrigue">''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]''</ref> [[Colonist/Legends|colonized]] the planet and became known as the [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo]].<ref name="GB" /> ''Mother Vima'' was later mentioned in a [[The New Essential Chronology (in-universe)|historical chronicle]] published by the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] [[Historical Council]] in [[36 ABY]].<ref name="NEC" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Sunrider.png|thumb|right|250px|Vima Sunrider, whose name is referenced in both the English and French names of ''Mother Vima'']]
''Mother Vima'' was first mentioned in the DataBank entry in the [[2001]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''.<ref name="GB" /> In the [[Wikipedia:French language|French]] edition of the game, the starship was named ''Mother Sunrider''.<ref name="French">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' [[Wikipedia:French language|French]] edition</ref> Both names reference<ref name="GB" /> the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] character [[Vima Sunrider]], who was introduced in the [[Tales of the Jedi 3|third issue]] of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic-book]] series, written by [[Tom Veitch]]<ref name="Tales">[[Tales of the Jedi 3|''Tales of the Jedi'' 3]]</ref> and published on [[December 21]], [[1993]].<ref>{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54568/star_wars_tales_of_the_jedi_1993_3|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (1993) #3}}</ref> [[In-universe]], Sunrider was active in the century prior to the expedition from Grizmallt and was perceived as the greatest Jedi of her age.<ref name="NEC" />

The [[StarWars.com]] episode guide for "[[Crisis on Naboo]]," the eighteenth episode of the [[The Clone Wars: Season Four|fourth season]] of ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'',<ref name="Crisis">{{SW|url=explore/the-clone-wars/ep418|text="Crisis on Naboo" - ''The Clone Wars'' Episode Guide|nolive=1}}"</ref> which aired on [[February 10]], [[2012]],<ref name="Ep guide">''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Episode Guide]]''</ref> stated that one of Grizmallt's colony ships crashed on Naboo, but it was not specified which vessel met this fate.<ref name="Crisis" />
